ogTitle: Cotherstone vs Cotswold Hills: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cotherstone and Cotswold Hills.
heading: Cotherstone vs Cotswold Hills — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cotherstone, Queensland and Cotswold Hills, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cotherstone has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Cotswold Hills (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cotherstone or Cotswold Hills safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cotherstone has a flood risk score of 30/100, while Cotswold Hills scores 57/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cotherstone scores 34/100 for bushfire risk. Cotswold Hills scores 37/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cotherstone or Cotswold Hills?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cotherstone has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.35% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
